channel.title: NPR Topics: Education
channel.description: We've been to school. We know how education works. Right? In fact, many aspects of learning — in homes, at schools, at work and elsewhere — are evolving rapidly, along with our understanding of learning. Join us as we explore how learning happens.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How young people feel about American identity, on the nation's 250th birthday
item.description: Listen to the best student podcasts about what "life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness" means for young people today.
item.content:encoded: Listen to the best student podcasts about what "life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness" means for young people today.
item.dc:creator: Janet W. Lee

item.title: Federal civil rights data holds schools accountable. Under Trump, it's 6 months late
item.description: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.
item.content:encoded: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: 'Workforce Pell' kicks off, but colleges find that few programs qualify
item.description: July 1 marks the official opening of a program that allows federal dollars to go toward short-term workforce training programs. But so far, just 12 states have created road maps for colleges to apply.
item.content:encoded: July 1 marks the official opening of a program that allows federal dollars to go toward short-term workforce training programs. But so far, just 12 states have created road maps for colleges to apply.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Kids with autism are prone to drowning. Florida is trying to prevent that
item.description: Kids with autism can be 160 times more likely than other children to drown. Florida is dedicating state money to a program that prioritizes swim lessons for these kids.
item.content:encoded:
Kids with autism can be 160 times more likely than other children to drown. Florida is dedicating state money to a program that prioritizes swim lessons for these kids.

(Image credit: LA Johnson)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: 'Workforce Pell' kicks off, but colleges find that few programs qualify
item.description: July 1 marks the official opening of a program that allows federal dollars to go toward short-term workforce training programs. So far just 12 states have created road maps for colleges to apply.
item.content:encoded:
July 1 marks the official opening of a program that allows federal dollars to go toward short-term workforce training programs. So far just 12 states have created road maps for colleges to apply.

(Image credit: Annelise Capossela for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Texas will require students to read Bible passages
item.description: Texas students will be required to read Bible passages and learn about the role of Christianity in the state's history under new reading lists and social studies curriculum approved on Friday
item.content:encoded: Texas students will be required to read Bible passages and learn about the role of Christianity in the state's history under new reading lists and social studies curriculum approved on Friday
item.dc:creator: Bill Zeeble

item.title: Will the new student loan limits actually drive down tuition? Economists weigh in
item.description: The idea that there's a connection between federal student loans and what colleges charge dates back almost four decades. But it's unclear that link can lead to lower costs.
item.content:encoded:
The idea that there's a connection between federal student loans and what colleges charge dates back almost four decades. But it's unclear that link can lead to lower costs.

(Image credit: Eduardo Munoz Alvarez)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Big changes coming to student loans on July 1
item.description: Starting on July 1, the federal government will make some big changes to how student loans can be repaid or forgiven.
item.content:encoded: Starting on July 1, the federal government will make some big changes to how student loans can be repaid or forgiven.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Once designed to strip Native students of culture, this school now safeguards it
item.description: For more than a century, federal boarding schools worked to forcibly assimilate Native American children into white culture. Here's how one Santa Fe school has worked to change that legacy.
item.content:encoded:
For more than a century, federal boarding schools worked to forcibly assimilate Native American children into white culture. Here's how one Santa Fe school has worked to change that legacy.

(Image credit: Esha Chiocchio for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Student loan borrowers will get an interest rate cut if they sign up for auto pay
item.description: The Trump administration wants to jumpstart student loan repayment, with federal student loan debt approaching $2 trillion.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration wants to jumpstart student loan repayment, with federal student loan debt approaching $2 trillion.

(Image credit: Daniel de la Hoz)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner
